steps.stepTitle: Identify the Need for a DPIA
steps.stepDescription: Explaining why this DPIA matters and who is responsible

questions.label: Project Title & Description
questions.placeholder: What are you trying to achieve?
questions.description: Provide a clear title and concise description of your data processing project. Include the main objectives, scope, and key stakeholders involved.

questions.label: Why was a DPIA needed?

options.label: High-risk processing
options.description: Processing that could pose high risks to individuals' rights and freedoms

options.label: Special category data
options.description: Processing involving sensitive data (ethnicity, religion, health, etc.)

options.label: Large-scale processing
options.description: Processing of large amounts of personal data systematically

options.label: Processing involves children
options.description: Processing of personal data of children (under 16)

questions.description: Select the primary reason(s) that triggered the need for a DPIA under Article 35 of GDPR. You may have multiple reasons.

questions.label: Related Documents
questions.description: Provide links or references to relevant project documentation, such as the project proposal, system design, security review, or business case.

steps.stepDescription: How you collect, use, store, and delete data

questions.label: Data Flow & Description
questions.placeholder: How is data collected, used, stored, deleted?
questions.description: Describe the complete data lifecycle: where data is collected from, how it flows through systems, how it's used, stored, and ultimately deleted or archived. Include all systems and processes involved.

questions.label: Data Retention Period
questions.description: Specify how long personal data will be retained. This should be based on legal requirements and business necessity. Shorter retention periods are preferred under data minimization principles.

questions.label: Who has access?
questions.description: List all parties who have access to the personal data, including internal teams (HR, Finance, IT), external service providers, contractors, and any third parties. Specify what access level each party has.

questions.label: Is data shared with anyone?
questions.placeholder: If yes, with whom and why?
questions.description: If personal data is transferred to third parties (vendors, partners, other organizations), describe with whom, for what purpose, and under what legal basis. Include any data transfer mechanisms (e.g., contracts, Standard Contractual Clauses).

steps.stepDescription: Identify who to consult and document their input

questions.label: Who did you consult?
questions.description: List all stakeholders consulted during the DPIA process. This should include your Data Protection Officer, security and IT teams, legal department, business owners, and potentially data subjects if appropriate.

questions.label: How and when?
questions.description: Describe the consultation methods used (meetings, surveys, workshops, email) and provide dates. Document how feedback was collected and the level of engagement from each stakeholder.

questions.label: Their concerns & recommendations
questions.description: Summarize the key concerns, recommendations, and issues raised by stakeholders during consultation. Explain how these have been addressed in the DPIA.

steps.stepDescription: Ensure processing is justified and data-minimized

questions.label: Lawful basis

options.label: Consent
options.description: Explicit consent has been given by data subjects

options.label: Contract
options.description: Processing is necessary to perform a contract with the data subject

options.label: Legal Obligation
options.description: Processing is required by law or regulation

options.label: Legitimate Interests
options.description: Processing is necessary for legitimate business interests

questions.description: Select the primary legal basis under GDPR Article 6 that justifies this processing. The lawful basis must be documented and communicated to data subjects.

questions.label: How are you minimizing data collection?
questions.placeholder: Only collecting necessary data? Anonymization? Retention limits?
questions.description: Explain how you apply data minimization principles: collecting only data that is necessary for the stated purpose, using anonymization or pseudonymization where possible, and limiting retention periods.

questions.label: How will you support individuals' rights?
questions.description: Identify which data subject rights you will support. At minimum, you must provide mechanisms for individuals to access their data and request deletion (right to erasure) where applicable.

steps.stepDescription: List potential harms and assess likelihood and severity

questions.label: Risk Assessment Table

columns.label: Risk Description
columns.description: What is the potential harm or security/privacy incident?

columns.label: Likelihood
columns.description: How probable is this risk? (Remote, Possible, Probable)

columns.label: Severity
columns.description: How severe would the impact be if this occurred? (Minimal, Significant, Severe)

columns.label: Overall Risk
columns.description: Overall risk level combining likelihood and severity (Low, Medium, High)

questions.description: Identify potential risks to individuals' rights and freedoms. Consider both deliberate attacks (e.g., data breaches) and accidental events (e.g., system failures). Assess each risk on likelihood and severity.

steps.stepDescription: For each medium or high risk, add mitigation measures

questions.label: Risk Mitigation Table

columns.label: Risk
columns.description: Which risk from Step 5 are you addressing?

columns.label: Proposed Measure
columns.description: What technical, organizational, or legal control will you implement?

columns.label: Effect
columns.description: How does this measure reduce the risk?

columns.label: Residual Risk
columns.description: What risk remains after implementing this measure?

columns.label: Timeline
columns.description: When will this measure be implemented?

questions.description: For each medium or high risk identified in Step 5, propose specific measures to reduce it. Include technical controls (encryption, access controls), organizational controls (training, policies), and legal controls (contracts). Document the timeline for implementation.

questions.label: DPO Name
questions.description: Full name of the Data Protection Officer providing approval

questions.label: DPO Email
questions.description: Email address of the DPO for audit trail and future reference

questions.label: DPO Advice
questions.description: The DPO's formal advice on the DPIA, including any concerns, recommendations, or conditions for processing to proceed

questions.label: Owner Name
questions.description: Full name of the business owner or senior manager approving this processing

questions.label: Owner Email
questions.description: Email address of the business owner for audit trail

questions.label: Do you accept the residual risks?
questions.description: Confirm whether the business owner accepts any remaining risks after mitigation measures are implemented

questions.label: Can processing proceed?
questions.description: The final decision on whether processing can proceed: Yes (unconditionally), Conditional (subject to implementing measures), or No (processing must not proceed)

questions.label: Next review date
questions.description: When will this DPIA be reviewed again? Typically annually or when processing scope changes significantly
